english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Colossians

verses.latin_vulgate: Domini, quod justum est et æquum, servis præstate: scientes quod et vos Dominum habetis in cælo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ κύριοι τὸ δίκαιον καὶ τὴν ἰσότητα τοῖς δούλοις παρέχεσθε εἰδότες ὅτι καὶ ὑμεῖς ἔχετε κύριον ἐν οὐρανοῖς
verses.king_james: Masters, give unto *your* servants that which is just and equal; knowing that ye also have a Master in heaven.

verses.latin_vulgate: Orationi instate, vigilantes in ea in gratiarum actione:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῇ προσευχῇ προσκαρτερεῖτε γρηγοροῦντες ἐν αὐτῇ ἐν εὐχαριστίᾳ
verses.king_james: Continue in prayer, and watch in the same with thanksgiving;

verses.latin_vulgate: orantes simul et pro nobis, ut Deus aperiat nobis ostium sermonis ad loquendum mysterium Christi (propter quod etiam vinctus sum),
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προσευχόμενοι ἅμα καὶ περὶ ἡμῶν ἵνα ὁ θεὸς ἀνοίξῃ ἡμῖν θύραν τοῦ λόγου λαλῆσαι τὸ μυστήριον τοῦ χριστοῦ δι᾽ ὃ καὶ δέδεμαι
verses.king_james: Withal praying also for us, that God would open unto us a door of utterance, to speak the mystery of Christ, for which I am also in bonds:

verses.latin_vulgate: ut manifestem illud ita ut oportet me loqui.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα φανερώσω αὐτὸ ὡς δεῖ με λαλῆσαι
verses.king_james: That I may make it manifest, as I ought to speak.

verses.latin_vulgate: In sapientia ambulate ad eos, qui foris sunt: tempus redimentes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν σοφίᾳ περιπατεῖτε πρὸς τοὺς ἔξω τὸν καιρὸν ἐξαγοραζόμενοι
verses.king_james: Walk in wisdom toward them that are without, redeeming the time.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sermo vester semper in gratia sale sit conditus, ut sciatis quomodo oporteat vos unicuique respondere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ λόγος ὑμῶν πάντοτε ἐν χάριτι ἅλατι ἠρτυμένος εἰδέναι πῶς δεῖ ὑμᾶς ἑνὶ ἑκάστῳ ἀποκρίνεσθαι
verses.king_james: Let your speech *be* alway with grace, seasoned with salt, that ye may know how ye ought to answer every man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ circa me sunt, omnia vobis nota faciet Tychicus, carissimus frater, et fidelis minister, et conservus in Domino:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὰ κατ᾽ ἐμὲ πάντα γνωρίσει ὑμῖν τυχικὸς ὁ ἀγαπητὸς ἀδελφὸς καὶ πιστὸς διάκονος καὶ σύνδουλος ἐν κυρίῳ
verses.king_james: All my state shall Tychicus declare unto you, *who is* a beloved brother, and a faithful minister and fellowservant in the Lord:

verses.latin_vulgate: quem misi ad vos ad hoc ipsum, ut cognoscat quæ circa vos sunt, et consoletur corda vestra,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃν ἔπεμψα πρὸς ὑμᾶς εἰς αὐτὸ τοῦτο ἵνα γνῷ τὰ περὶ ὑμῶν καὶ παρακαλέσῃ τὰς καρδίας ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: Whom I have sent unto you for the same purpose, that he might know your estate, and comfort your hearts;

verses.latin_vulgate: cum Onesimo carissimo, et fideli fratre, qui ex vobis est. Omnia, quæ hic aguntur, nota facient vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σὺν ὀνησίμῳ τῷ πιστῷ καὶ ἀγαπητῷ ἀδελφῷ ὅς ἐστιν ἐξ ὑμῶν πάντα ὑμῖν γνωριοῦσιν τὰ ὧδε
verses.king_james: With Onesimus, a faithful and beloved brother, who is *one* of you. They shall make known unto you all things which *are done* here.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutat vos Aristarchus concaptivus meus, et Marcus consobrinus Barnabæ, de quo accepistis mandata: si venerit ad vos, excipite illum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσπάζεται ὑμᾶς ἀρίσταρχος ὁ συναιχμάλωτός μου καὶ μᾶρκος ὁ ἀνεψιὸς βαρναβᾶ περὶ οὗ ἐλάβετε ἐντολάς ἐὰν ἔλθῃ πρὸς ὑμᾶς δέξασθε αὐτόν
verses.king_james: Aristarchus my fellowprisoner saluteth you, and Marcus, sister's son to Barnabas, (touching whom ye received commandments: if he come unto you, receive him;)

verses.latin_vulgate: et Jesus, qui dicitur Justus: qui sunt ex circumcisione: hi soli sunt adjutores mei in regno Dei, qui mihi fuerunt solatio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰησοῦς ὁ λεγόμενος ἰοῦστος οἱ ὄντες ἐκ περιτομῆς οὗτοι μόνοι συνεργοὶ εἰς τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ θεοῦ οἵτινες ἐγενήθησάν μοι παρηγορία
verses.king_james: And Jesus, which is called Justus, who are of the circumcision. These only *are my* fellowworkers unto the kingdom of God, which have been a comfort unto me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutat vos Epaphras, qui ex vobis est, servus Christi Jesu, semper sollicitus pro vobis in orationibus, ut stetis perfecti, et pleni in omni voluntate Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσπάζεται ὑμᾶς ἐπαφρᾶς ὁ ἐξ ὑμῶν δοῦλος χριστοῦ πάντοτε ἀγωνιζόμενος ὑπὲρ ὑμῶν ἐν ταῖς προσευχαῖς ἵνα στῆτε τέλειοι καὶ πεπληρωμένοι ἐν παντὶ θελήματι τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: Epaphras, who is *one* of you, a servant of Christ, saluteth you, always labouring fervently for you in prayers, that ye may stand perfect and complete in all the will of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Testimonium enim illi perhibeo quod habet multum laborem pro vobis, et pro iis qui sunt Laodiciæ, et qui Hierapoli.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μαρτυρῶ γὰρ αὐτῷ ὅτι ἔχει ζῆλον πολὺν ὑπὲρ ὑμῶν καὶ τῶν ἐν λαοδικείᾳ καὶ τῶν ἐν ἱεραπόλει
verses.king_james: For I bear him record, that he hath a great zeal for you, and them *that are* in Laodicea, and them in Hierapolis.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutat vos Lucas, medicus carissimus, et Demas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσπάζεται ὑμᾶς λουκᾶς ὁ ἰατρὸς ὁ ἀγαπητὸς καὶ δημᾶς
verses.king_james: Luke, the beloved physician, and Demas, greet you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutate fratres, qui sunt Laodiciæ, et Nympham, et quæ in domo ejus est, ecclesiam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀσπάσασθε τοὺς ἐν λαοδικείᾳ ἀδελφοὺς καὶ νύμφαν καὶ τὴν κατ᾽ οἶκον αὐτοῦ ἐκκλησίαν
verses.king_james: Salute the brethren which are in Laodicea, and Nymphas, and the church which is in his house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum lecta fuerit apud vos epistola hæc, facite ut et in Laodicensium ecclesia legatur: et eam, quæ Laodicensium est, vos legatis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὅταν ἀναγνωσθῇ παρ᾽ ὑμῖν ἡ ἐπιστολή ποιήσατε ἵνα καὶ ἐν τῇ λαοδικέων ἐκκλησίᾳ ἀναγνωσθῇ καὶ τὴν ἐκ λαοδικείας ἵνα καὶ ὑμεῖς ἀναγνῶτε
verses.king_james: And when this epistle is read among you, cause that it be read also in the church of the Laodiceans; and that ye likewise read the *epistle* from Laodicea.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dicite Archippo: Vide ministerium, quod accepisti in Domino, ut illud impleas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἴπατε ἀρχίππῳ βλέπε τὴν διακονίαν ἣν παρέλαβες ἐν κυρίῳ ἵνα αὐτὴν πληροῖς
verses.king_james: And say to Archippus, Take heed to the ministry which thou hast received in the Lord, that thou fulfil it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salutatio, mea manu Pauli. Memores estote vinculorum meorum. Gratia vobiscum. Amen.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ ἀσπασμὸς τῇ ἐμῇ χειρὶ παύλου μνημονεύετέ μου τῶν δεσμῶν ἡ χάρις μεθ᾽ ὑμῶν ἀμήν
verses.king_james: The salutation by the hand of me Paul. Remember my bonds. Grace *be* with you. Amen.
